title: Say It Right: Talk to AI

modules.title: The Magic Words That Aren't Magic
modules.sub: Discover why vague instructions leave AI totally lost
modules.description: Learners meet Pixel and try giving simple instructions, only to watch Pixel do something unexpected or unhelpful. Through funny trial-and-error moments, they figure out that clear words — not wishful thinking — are what actually make AI work.

modules.title: Be Specific: A Treasure Hunt Game
modules.sub: Use precise clues to guide Pixel to hidden treasure
modules.description: Students play a treasure hunt where each clue they write for Pixel must be specific enough to lead somewhere useful. They compare vague instructions against detailed ones and see the difference in Pixel's results firsthand.

modules.title: Asking Again When AI Gets Confused
modules.sub: Learn to rephrase, retry, and rescue a stuck conversation
modules.description: When Pixel misunderstands a prompt, learners practice rewriting their instructions in a new way instead of giving up. They explore why changing even a few words can completely change what AI understands and produces.

modules.title: Giving AI a Character to Play
modules.sub: Set the scene so Pixel becomes whoever you need
modules.description: Learners experiment with role prompts, telling Pixel to respond as a pirate, a scientist, or a friendly chef. They discover how framing and context shape AI responses, and they design their own character prompt for a creative mini-challenge.

modules.title: Prompts That Help vs. Prompts That Trick
modules.sub: Spot the difference between helpful asks and sneaky ones
modules.description: Students examine pairs of prompts and decide which ones are fair, useful, and honest versus which ones try to trick or misuse AI. This module builds early critical thinking about responsible prompting and why the intention behind a prompt matters.

modules.title: The Big Prompt Challenge
modules.sub: Put every skill together in one final creative mission
modules.description: Learners tackle a multi-step challenge where they must write, test, and refine a series of prompts to help Pixel complete a real creative task from start to finish. They reflect on what made their prompts successful and share their best work with the group.

synopsis: Meet Pixel, an AI who only does exactly what you say â and nothing more. In this playful adventure, you help Pixel complete missions by learning to write clearer, smarter, more creative instructions, discovering along the way that talking to AI is a real skill anyone can learn.
rationale: Children aged 8â10 are increasingly encountering AI assistants at home and at school, but without any guided introduction to how their language shapes AI responses, they develop either fear or uncritical over-trust from their earliest interactions.
learning_outcome: Students can write clear, specific, and creative prompts that produce useful AI outputs, and can explain in simple terms why the words they choose change the results they get.
nearest_existing: What Does AI Actually Do With Words?
